data.title: AMD dips after unveiling new AI chip, CPU at showcase event
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices' (NASDAQ:AMD) stock fell about 4% on Thursday amid CEO Lisa Su unveiling new artificial intelligence processors at its Advancing AI 2024 event in San Francisco on Thursday, stepping up efforts to challenge market leader Nvidia (NVDA).

The company showed off its new Turin EPYC data center CPUs and an Instinct MI325x AI accelerator. Su also unveiled Ryzen AI PRO 300 Series, the first Microsoft (MSFT) Copilot + laptops designed for enterprise, according to the company.

AMD touted MI325x to be better than Nvidia's H200 HGX on several parameters. Meanwhile, the company called the fifth generation AMD EPYC the world's best CPU for Cloud, Enterprise and AI.

AMD noted that the EPYC 5th Gen 9965 is the industry's highest performing server CPU, by showing that it is better than the company's 4th Gen 9754 and Intel's (INTC) Xeon 5th Gen 8592+.

At the Computex 2024 show in Taipei in June, the company introduced its latest AI processors and provided a roadmap to develop AI chips over the next two years. Su had unveiled the AMD Instinct MI325X accelerator, and the AMD Instinct MI350 series accelerators based on AMD CDNA 4 architecture.

At the Advancing AI event, AMD reiterated its commitment to the GPU roadmap, which includes MI325X in 2024, MI350 in the second half of 2025, and MI400 in 2026. These would potentially compete with Nvidia's upcoming Blackwell GPUs. At the Computex event, AMD had revealed plans to deliver performance and memory leadership on an annual basis for generative AI, similar to plans by rival Nvidia (NVDA) to shorten its release cycle to an annual basis.

Su noted that data center AI accelerator market is expected to grow to $500B in 2028 from the $45B it was in 2023, a growth of 60% CAGR. She had previously forecast the market to be $400B in 2027.

AMD also invited several speakers from its partners, including tech giants Microsoft (MSFT), Meta Platforms (META), and Oracle (ORCL).

Kevin Salvadore, VP of Infrastructure and Engineering at Meta, noted that the company has deployed over 1.5 million EPYC CPUs.

The race to develop generative AI products has resulted in a rising demand for the advanced chips used in AI data centers. Companies developing large language models, or LLMs, rely on advanced processors to train these models as they require high computational power, among other things.

data.title: Kyverna, Cabaletta started at buys by UBS on CAR T therapy potential
data.description:
UBS initiated coverage of cell therapy developers Kyverna Therapeutics (NASDAQ:KYTX) and Cabaletta Bio (NASDAQ:CABA) with buy ratings, asserting that recent weakness in the stocks is overdone.

“Given the vast value proposition offering by a potentially curative therapy where there remains much unmet need, we see a significantly underappreciated market for severe autoimmune patients that are refractory to standard of care,” UBS said in a note.

The bank said while it “acknowledges durability and safety remain key debates on CAR Ts applications into autoimmunes, we view recent weakness over the last several months as overdone.”

UBS said Kyverna (KYTX), Cabaletta (CABA) and Bristol Myers’ (BMY) clinical experiences have validated the short-term efficacy of anti-CD19 CAR Ts in autoimmune. It sees 2025 and 2026 as pivotal years for the programs to provide clinical validations for long-term efficacy and safety.

The bank noted that both Bristol Myers (BMY) and Cabaletta (CABA) will be presenting “more clinical validations” at the American College of Rheumatology meeting in November.

UBS set a price target of $13 for Kyverna (KYTX) and $10 for Cabaletta (CABA).

data.title: Disney, Comcast, and SeaWorld to reopen Florida theme parks after Hurricane Milton landfall
data.description:
Disney (NYSE:DIS), Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA), and SeaWorld (NYSE:PRKS) expect to reopen their theme parks in Florida on Friday following the aftermath of Hurricane Milton in the U.S. state.

Disney, in an email statement, said they "may reopen" this Friday, and Comcast CEO Brian Roberts, speaking at the Bloomberg Screentime event, said the theme parks will reopen on Friday.

SeaWorld will reopen its Orlando parks this Friday but said its Spooktacular and Howl-O-Scream events will be canceled for that day.

data.title: Maris-Tech is expanding its operations in the U.S.
data.description:
• Maris-Tech (NASDAQ:MTEK) has announced a major expansion into the U.S. market with the establishment of its new subsidiary, Maris North America.
  • The HLS market is predicted to be worth $264.98B by 2034 at a CAGR of 6.01% from 2024 to 2034, and the defense market is forecasted to grow by $169.43B at a CAGR of 4.91% between 2023 and 2028.
  • Maris North America will serve as the strategic hub for Maris-Tech's operations across the U.S., further enhancing its ability to deliver localized support and forge new business relationships in North America.
  • "We expect our U.S. presence to boost our sales efforts and enable us to deliver more tailored, localized support to our growing customer base across North America." said Israel Bar, CEO.

data.title: Ormat Technologies secures land parcels in Nevada's BLM auction
data.description:
• Ormat Technologies (NYSE:ORA) has successfully secured multiple land parcels in Nevada’s Annual Bureau of Land Management Auction.
  • These lease acquisitions will significantly support Ormat's ongoing exploration and expansion efforts in the state, further strengthening the company's commitment to advancing renewable energy solutions and meeting Nevada's increasing demand for sustainable energy.
  • The newly leased parcels hold substantial potential for geothermal energy production, including a new greenfield prospect, an expansion opportunity for an existing operational asset, and several additional parcels that will enhance Ormat’s land position on an existing greenfield prospect.
  • "Our team is dedicated to exploring and developing these resources to their fullest potential, providing reliable and eco-friendly energy to the people of Nevada." said Doron Blachar, CEO.

data.title: Eli Lilly widens obesity reach with $1.4B KeyBioscience deal
OnKure started at outperform by Oppenheimer, OKI-219 potential cited
data.description:
• Oppenheimer started coverage of OnKure Therapeutics (NASDAQ:OKUR) with an outperform rating, citing the potential of its breast cancer drug candidate OKI-219.
  • The investment bank said it believes OKI-219 could become the “go-to option for one of the most frequent mutations in breast cancer” due in part to its selectivity as a PI3Ka inhibitor.
  • Oppenheimer said while the drug would enter a crowded market, it believes OKI-219’s high selectivity will make it more tolerable and, hence, more easily combinable with other therapies.
  • “In a world where triplet or even quadruplet therapies could be the norm, we think combinability will be king,” the bank added.
  • Oppenheimer set its price target for the stock at $35.

data.title: Akebia gains additional CMS reimbursement for end-stage renal disease drug Vafseo
data.description:
• CMS has determined that Akebia Therapeutics' (NASDAQ:AKBA) Vafseo (vadadustat) meets criteria for the Transitional Drug Add-On Payment Adjustment (TDAPA) for anemia management of end-stage renal disease starting on Jan. 1, 2025.
  • TDAPA status, part of the prospective payment system functional category, provides for two years of reimbursement for Vafseo plus the ESRD bundled rate to dialysis organizations.
  • TDAPA is designed to provide newer drugs to Medicare patients by proving additional payments to absorb the higher costs.
  • Also, Akebia was given a Level II Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS) code for Vafseo for billing by dialysis organizations for Medicare enrollees.

data.title: Castor Maritime announces delivery of the M/V Magic Ariel
data.description:
• Castor Maritime (NASDAQ:CTRM) has announced that on Oct. 9, 2024, it took delivery of the M/V Magic Ariel, the 2020-built Kamsarmax bulk carrier vessel it had agreed to acquire as previously announced on Sept. 30, 2024.
  • The vessel acquisition was financed in its entirety with cash on hand.
  • The M/V Magic Ariel will be employed under a time charter contract with a minimum duration of about seven months at a gross daily rate equal to 108% of the Baltic Panamax Index 5TC (BPI5TC).

data.title: Atlanta Fed's Bostic is 'definitely open' to skipping rate cut in November - report
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta President Raphael Bostic said Thursday he was "definitely open" to holding interest rates steady at the central bank's November gathering, as disinflation appeared to stall with the latest CPI data.

Earlier on Thursday, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics released a fresh read on the closely-watched Consumer Price Index, which rose more than expected M/M in September, signaling inflation risks remain. That's on top of the surprisingly strong September jobs report.

“I think we have the ability to be patient and wait and let things play out a little longer…. There are elements of today’s [CPI] report which I think validate that view,” Bostic said in an interview with the Wall Street Journal.

Calling the CPI report "janky," Bostic said he is "open to not moving at one of the last two meeting [this year] if the data comes in as I expect," he added. Bostic, who has penciled in a total of 75 basis points worth of easing this year, has long anticipated a bumpy disinflation trend.

It was only at the end of last month when Bostic said he'd be willing to support another 50-bp cut at the November meeting.

Then again, a case could be made to cut rates at either or both remaining meetings, he said. "It's a journey to get to neutral and the nuance of, 'Do you move 25 basis points here? 50 basis points there?' I don't think those are as consequential."
